title: Unable to install buildah on Amazon Linux 2
body:
**Description**

I tried to install buildah on a fresh ec2 instance with Amazon Linux 2 by following the official documentation (https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/install.md). 

Installing buildah rpm fails with a missing dependency on crun.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Start a fresh ec2 instance with amazon linux 2
2. Follow install guide https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/install.md


**Describe the results you received:**

```
$ sudo yum install buildah -y
Loaded plugins: copr, extras_suggestions, langpacks, priorities, update-motd                                                                                                                                                                                    1 packages excluded due to repository priority protections                                                                                                                                                                                        Resolving Dependencies                                                                                                                                                                      --> Running transaction check
---> Package buildah.x86_64 0:1.15.0-1.el7 will be installed
--> Processing Dependency: slirp4netns >= 0.3-0 for package: buildah-1.15.0-1.el7.x86_64
--> Processing Dependency: crun >= 0.10-1 for package: buildah-1.15.0-1.el7.x86_64
--> Processing Dependency: runc for package: buildah-1.15.0-1.el7.x86_64
--> Processing Dependency: containers-common for package: buildah-1.15.0-1.el7.x86_64                                                                                                                                                                                          --> Processing Dependency: container-selinux for package: buildah-1.15.0-1.el7.x86_64
--> Running transaction check
---> Package buildah.x86_64 0:1.15.0-1.el7 will be installed
--> Processing Dependency: crun >= 0.10-1 for package: buildah-1.15.0-1.el7.x86_64
---> Package container-selinux.noarch 2:2.107-3.el7 will be installed
---> Package containers-common.x86_64 2:1.0.0-1.el7 will be installed
---> Package runc.x86_64 0:1.0.0-0.1.20200204.gitdc9208a.amzn2 will be installed
---> Package slirp4netns.x86_64 0:1.1.1-1.el7 will be installed
--> Processing Dependency: libslirp.so.0(SLIRP_4.1)(64bit) for package: slirp4netns-1.1.1-1.el7.x86_64
--> Processing Dependency: libslirp.so.0(SLIRP_4.0)(64bit) for package: slirp4netns-1.1.1-1.el7.x86_64
--> Processing Dependency: libslirp.so.0()(64bit) for package: slirp4netns-1.1.1-1.el7.x86_64
--> Running transaction check
---> Package buildah.x86_64 0:1.15.0-1.el7 will be installed
--> Processing Dependency: crun >= 0.10-1 for package: buildah-1.15.0-1.el7.x86_64
---> Package libslirp.x86_64 0:4.2.0-2.el7 will be installed
--> Finished Dependency Resolution                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             Error: Package: buildah-1.15.0-1.el7.x86_64 (devel_kubic_libcontainers_stable)
           Requires: crun >= 0.10-1
You could try using --skip-broken to work around the problem
You could try running: rpm -Va --nofiles --nodigest
```
**Describe the results you expected:**

Buildah should be installed

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
package buildah is not installed
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
buildah not installed
```

**Output of `podman version` if reporting a `podman build` issue:**

```
podman not installed
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
$ cat /etc/*release
NAME="Amazon Linux"
VERSION="2"
ID="amzn"
ID_LIKE="centos rhel fedora"
VERSION_ID="2"
PRETTY_NAME="Amazon Linux 2"
ANSI_COLOR="0;33"
CPE_NAME="cpe:2.3:o:amazon:amazon_linux:2"
HOME_URL="https://amazonlinux.com/"
Amazon Linux release 2 (Karoo)
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
$ uname -a
Linux ip-A-B-C-D.eu-central-1.compute.internal 4.14.181-140.257.amzn2.x86_64 #1 SMP Wed May 27 02:17:36 UTC 2020 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
cat: /etc/containers/storage.conf: No such file or directory
```

comments.body: Where is this buildah rpm from?  There is no crun in RHEL7, so it should not exist in a RHEL repo

I dont know, but I think its related to the buildah repo.

```
# yum deplist buildah
Loaded plugins: copr, extras_suggestions, langpacks, priorities, update-motd
1 packages excluded due to repository priority protections
package: buildah.x86_64 1.15.0-1.el7
  dependency: container-selinux
   provider: container-selinux.noarch 2:2.107-3.el7
  dependency: containers-common
   provider: containers-common.x86_64 2:1.0.0-1.el7
  dependency: crun >= 0.10-1
   Unsatisfied dependency
  dependency: libassuan.so.0()(64bit)
   provider: libassuan.x86_64 2.1.0-3.amzn2.0.2
  dependency: libc.so.6(GLIBC_2.14)(64bit)
   provider: glibc.x86_64 2.26-34.amzn2
  dependency: libdevmapper.so.1.02()(64bit)
   provider: device-mapper-libs.x86_64 7:1.02.146-4.amzn2.0.2
  dependency: libdevmapper.so.1.02(Base)(64bit)
   provider: device-mapper-libs.x86_64 7:1.02.146-4.amzn2.0.2
  dependency: libdevmapper.so.1.02(DM_1_02_97)(64bit)
   provider: device-mapper-libs.x86_64 7:1.02.146-4.amzn2.0.2
  dependency: libgpg-error.so.0()(64bit)
   provider: libgpg-error.x86_64 1.12-3.amzn2.0.3
  dependency: libgpgme.so.11()(64bit)
   provider: gpgme.x86_64 1.3.2-5.amzn2.0.2
  dependency: libgpgme.so.11(GPGME_1.0)(64bit)
   provider: gpgme.x86_64 1.3.2-5.amzn2.0.2
  dependency: libgpgme.so.11(GPGME_1.1)(64bit)
   provider: gpgme.x86_64 1.3.2-5.amzn2.0.2
  dependency: libpthread.so.0()(64bit)
   provider: glibc.x86_64 2.26-34.amzn2
  dependency: libpthread.so.0(GLIBC_2.2.5)(64bit)
   provider: glibc.x86_64 2.26-34.amzn2
  dependency: libpthread.so.0(GLIBC_2.3.2)(64bit)
   provider: glibc.x86_64 2.26-34.amzn2
  dependency: libseccomp >= 2.4.1-0
   provider: libseccomp.x86_64 2.4.1-1.amzn2
   provider: libseccomp.i686 2.4.1-1.amzn2
  dependency: libseccomp.so.2()(64bit)
   provider: libseccomp.x86_64 2.4.1-1.amzn2
  dependency: rtld(GNU_HASH)
   provider: glibc.x86_64 2.26-34.amzn2
   provider: glibc.i686 2.26-34.amzn2
  dependency: runc
   provider: runc.x86_64 1.0.0-0.1.20200204.gitdc9208a.amzn2
   provider: docker.x86_64 18.06.1ce-10.amzn2
  dependency: slirp4netns >= 0.3-0
   provider: slirp4netns.x86_64 1.1.1-1.el7

# yum info buildah
Loaded plugins: copr, extras_suggestions, langpacks, priorities, update-motd
1 packages excluded due to repository priority protections
Available Packages
Name        : buildah
Arch        : x86_64
Version     : 1.15.0
Release     : 1.el7
Size        : 14 M
Repo        : devel_kubic_libcontainers_stable
Summary     : A command line tool used for creating OCI Images
URL         : https://buildah.io
License     : ASL 2.0
Description : The buildah package provides a command line tool which can be used to
            : * create a working container from scratch
            : or
            : * create a working container from an image as a starting point
            : * mount/umount a working container's root file system for manipulation
            : * save container's root file system layer to create a new image
            : * delete a working container or an image
````

Looks to be a specific issue with this release of the package that @lsm5 has addressed here:
https://build.opensuse.org/package/show/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable/buildah#comment-1258358

The '-2' version of the package built cleanly, and is available here:
https://build.opensuse.org/package/binaries/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable/buildah/CentOS_7

Clearing the yum cache in the Amazon deployed image and installing again should resolve the issue (if it's not resolved for new builds already).
Problem is solved ! Installation of podman, skopeo, buildah works fine now without having issues.
